arts.ttl: Kit Clash Analysis: Feyenoord vs Bayern Munich in Champions League
arts.sum: Yesterday's Champions League match between Feyenoord and Bayern Munich presented an interesting kit situation, though not a severe clash, thanks to contrasting shorts and socks.

arts.ttl: Red handed: Sven Ulreich red suspends him for next Bayern Munich Champions League match
arts.sum: To really put the icing on the cake from a Bayern perspective, shortly before the full-time whistle had blown, backup keeper Sven Ulreich was shown a red card, which is presumed to have come from two consecutive yellows. The resulting red card was shown for complaining to the officials shortly after Feyenoord had scored their third goal in the 89th minute that sealed the deal for the home side.

arts.ttl: SC Freiburg vs. Bayern Munich: 2024/25 Bundesliga full coverage
arts.sum: SC Freiburg desperately needs to win to continue its fight for a top-six finish, while Bayern Munich surely does not want to give up any of its lead over Bayer Leverkusen. Earlier this season, Bayern Munich held off SC Freiburg 2-0, but this time figures to be more difficult for the Bavarians.

arts.ttl: “We wouldn’t have scored a goal even if we kept playing for another two hours” — Bayern Munich CEO chalks up Champions League trouncing to just one of those days
arts.sum: “We had imagined things differently today, but that’s part of football,” CEO Jan-Christian Dreesen said in post-match comments captured via @iMiaSanMia . Anyone who missed Bayern Munich ’s 3-0 humbling at the hands of Feyenoord on Wednesday was likely stunned into silence at the sight of the scoreline.

arts.ttl: Erik ten Hag and 5 Bayern Munich reserves head coaches you may have forgotten
arts.sum: Two years later, he stepped up to the role of reserves head coach and achieved a solid second-place finish in the 2017/18 term, with his team scoring a healthy 84 goals in the Regionalliga. Erik Ten Hag spent two years as Bayern Munich reserves head coach.
